text:
Terminology
Connection Rate Limiter
A token-bucket based system introduced in SPM-4319 to admit, reject or queue the connection
request (details). It aims to prevent connection spikes from overloading the system.
Operations Rate Limiter
A token bucket based system introduced in SPM-4209 to admit an operation or reject it with a
System Overload error at the front door of a mongod/s (details). It aims to prevent operations
spikes from overloading the system.
Load shedding in execution control
Backpressure-compliant driver
A driver implementing the upcoming Backpressure Specification to ensure it reacts to the rate
limiters in a way that minimizes server overload while optimizing throughput for the customer's
application.
Current driver
A driver that does not implement the Backpressure Specification.
Total Overload
A situation where the server is overloaded to the point of becoming unavailable and/or crash.
Operations or connection requests to the server will hang.
Partial Overload / Brownout
